{{Infobox technique
| name = Combat Art
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Combat Art Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A Combat Art is a specialized discipline used by Combat Artists to enhance their fighting capabilities through the manipulation of [[Aether]]. Unlike traditional magic, these arts are not intended to function as standalone spells and cannot be easily maintained for long durations. Because both [[Combat Arts]] and proper spellcasting require intense concentration and resonance with the [[Aether]], attempting to use them simultaneously is nearly impossible and often results in self-interference. While some masters apply these techniques to firearms, the effectiveness of a Combat Art with guns is often limited by the weapon's own mechanical specifications. These arts can be applied to various weapons, such as swords or force-blades, and are used for both offensive and defensive purposes. Offensive arts can manifest as devastating strikes, massive washes of flame, or even razor-sharp fields capable of shredding armor and destroying large objects. Defensive arts can create shields, deflect projectiles like missiles or bullets, or even reflect incoming attacks. The power of a Combat Art is deeply connected to the user's discipline, dedication, and their understanding of universal truths embedded within the [[Aether]]. While some species like the [[Aethril]] are naturally strong in magic, they often lack the physical prowess required for many [[Combat Arts]], whereas others, such as the [[Visiri]], possess immense strength that complements their combat skills.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Combat Art]] can summon a storm of blades from the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c15">''The Captain'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Combat Arts]] can be channeled through the ship spirit to empower weapons.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Combat Art is a discipline that requires the support of the [[Aether]] to be effective. While it can be utilized with extreme brutality, its manifestations vary, such as when a [[Karoshan]] [[force-blade]] invokes an eruption of golden energy. During his confrontation with [[Starhammer]], [[Raion Raithe]] employed a Combat Art that eventually faded into a thin flame. Additionally, it is suggested that the swords wielded by the [[Lichborn]] may constitute a form of Combat Art.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion]]'s [[Combat Art]] is useless without the support of the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Combat Art]] can be used with great brutality.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c11">''The Engineer'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Karoshan]]'s [[force-blade]] invokes a [[Combat Art]] that erupts in golden energy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14">''The Engineer'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion Raithe]] used a [[Combat Art]] during his fight with [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c37">''The Engineer'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Lichborn]]'s swords might be a [[Combat Art]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c43">''The Engineer'', chapter 43</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion Raithe]]'s [[Combat Art]] faded to a thin flame.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|During the conflict, the [[Red]] [[Knight]] employs Combat Art to successfully block an incoming attack. [[Albedon Pegarthus]] also utilizes the technique, as does [[Parryl]] during her combat engagement. The power of Combat Art is further demonstrated when the descent of a divine blade is identified as such a technique. Finally, [[Zyrana]] uses Combat Art to nudge her shots into their proper positions.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Red]] [[Knight]] uses [[Combat Art]] to block an attack.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20">''The Knight'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Albedon Pegarthus]] utilizes [[Combat Art]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c26">''The Knight'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Combat Art]] is a technique used by [[Parryl]] during her fight.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c31">''The Knight'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The descent of a divine blade was identified as a [[Combat Art]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35">''The Knight'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zyrana]] uses [[Combat Art]] to nudge her shots into position.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36">''The Knight'', chapter 36</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Shyrax incorporates Combat Art into her physical motions, causing her movements to crackle with energy. During a confrontation, the Combat Art of the [[Zenith Blade]] is powerful enough to knock [[Shadow Ark]] backwards. [[Aila]] also utilizes Combat Art, specifically manifesting a technique that emits an orange light from her blade. This orange Combat Art eventually slices into Shyrax's chest.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Shyrax uses [[Combat Art]] in her motions.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c6">''The Pilot'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Combat Art]] of the [[Zenith Blade]] knocked [[Shadow Ark]] backwards.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Shyrax's movement crackled with her [[Combat Art]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c21">''The Pilot'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila]] uses a [[Combat Art]] that emits orange light from her blade.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila]] uses [[Combat Art]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|An orange [[Combat Art]] sliced into Shyrax's chest.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c45">''The Pilot'', chapter 45</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Techniques]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
